summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #502 where adding an OpenAI-compatible provider without specifying a model caused a timeout error, and add regression tests to prevent recurrence.
summary.outcome: Discovered the fix was already on main (commits 67d6af6a and da754584). Added two regression tests: one proving the discovery path never falls through to the slow probing loop, and one for connection-refused scenarios. Committed, pushed, and opened a PR closing issue #502.

repo: Custom providers follow the Ollama pattern: when no model is specified, query /models directly (8s timeout) and return the list to UI for selection, rather than probing via /chat/completions.
The probing path could cause up to 80s worst-case latency (8 probes × 10s timeout each) for custom providers with no models in the hardcoded registry.
Provider setup lives in crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs; OpenAI-specific logic in crates/providers/src/openai.rs.
parse_models_payload filters non-chat models (e.g., dall-e-3 is excluded from the discovered model list).

code.finding: Added two regression tests: validate_key_custom_provider_does_not_probe_when_model_unset (mock server with instrumented /chat/completions that fails if called) and validate_key_custom_provider_connection_refused_returns_error.

workflow: Before writing a fix, always check git log to see if the issue was already addressed on main.
Strong regression tests for timeout/performance bugs use atomic flags on mock endpoints to assert code paths are never reached, not just that the result is correct.
rustfmt was run after editing the test file to keep formatting clean before committing.

friction: The fix for issue #502 was already on main but not linked to the issue, requiring investigation of recent commits to discover this — the issue was apparently not closed when the fix landed.
open_items: No test for the slow /models response case (near timeout threshold) — the current regression test only covers fast success and connection refused, not latency edge cases.
Issue #502 was open despite the fix already being on main; process gap around closing issues when fixes land.
